mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes corporate hiring and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decrease in unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong labor market, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signal higher discount rates, which reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which drives economic growth and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST rises by > 5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 240 signals inflationary pressures, prompting the Fed to raise rates, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates robust supply-side growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and GDPC1 on SPX returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During supply shocks, declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals reduced manufacturing activity, which lowers corporate profits and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where INDPRO declines by > 2% MoM.
